metadata.notes: daemon=bug: aider transcript carries the marker line, but the aider adapter has NO TaskEstimate scan (v1 is claudecode-only) and emits only a 200-rune-truncated AssistantText — record known_failing + file an issue. driver=ready (with a human prereq): the rig executes no recipe `setup` array (grep setup over scripts/ is empty — no instruction-file-seeding primitive), but aider uniquely loads a PERSISTENT user-level rule via ~/.aider.conf.yml `read: <persistent CONVENTIONS.md>`, and run-cell.sh isolates only IRRLICHT_HOME — never the agent's $HOME — so a once-seeded conventions file survives into the recording run. This is why aider stays ready while the opencode/pi siblings (which have only a per-run scratch path) are gap:seed_instruction.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug
driver_capability: ready

## Scenario meaning

While working a multi-phase task the agent emits its OWN progress estimate in-band, as a hidden HTML comment in its transcript-visible assistant text — `<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":N,"completed_rounds":M} -->` — driven by a persistent instruction-file rule (NO hook). irrlicht parses it read-only and projects `task_estimate` {total_rounds, completed_rounds, updated_at} + a `task_completion_eta` onto the session metrics WHILE the session is `working`, burning the ETA down as `completed_rounds` climbs, then dropping the fields once the session leaves `working`. A "round" is the agent's own unit; there is no daemon-side round counting. The user-observable signal: progress surfaces on the metrics while working (matching the reported values, non-decreasing, updated_at advancing), the ETA is monotone non-increasing, and the fields are suppressed at `ready`. State arc ready → working → ready.

## agent_supports: yes

Aider HAS a persistent-instruction mechanism: a conventions file loaded via `aider --read CONVENTIONS.md`, or the `.aider.conf.yml` `read:` entry, which loads on every run (aider.chat/docs/usage/conventions.html — the `--read` flag marks the file read-only; `read: CONVENTIONS.md` / `read: [..]` in the config loads it persistently). The marker is agent-agnostic plain text, so nothing about aider's surface blocks it from being emitted in assistant prose. The recipe can also fold the rule straight into the first prompt; the daemon parser does not care HOW the marker arrives, only that it lands in the transcript. Verdict `yes` because the mechanism exists and the marker is ordinary text the model can write — NOT a doc-stated guarantee that this model emits it (see caveat).

## daemon_capability: bug

This is the load-bearing finding and it is a PARSER bug, not an architectural incapability — the marker trace DOES exist and DOES reach the Source the daemon tails. Two cited blockers in the aider adapter:

1. **No marker scan.** `scanTaskEstimate` / `ev.TaskEstimate` is wired ONLY in the claudecode parser (core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/parser.go:389-390, taskestimate.go). The aider parser (core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go) never calls it and never sets `ParsedEvent.TaskEstimate`, so a working aider session emits NO `task_estimate` — every spec assertion (estimate present, monotone climb, ETA burndown, suppress-at-ready) fails.

2. **Assistant text is truncated before any scan could run.** Even if a scan were retrofitted at the obvious site, `Parser.closeModelCall` (parser.go:171-187) emits `AssistantText: truncate(text)` — `truncate` keeps only the last 200 runes (parser.go:266-273). claudecode deliberately scans the FULL block text precisely because `AssistantText` is tail-truncated and would lose early markers (taskestimate.go:34-38; parser.go:381-392). An aider scan would have to read the buffered full prose in `closeModelCall` before truncation, which today is discarded.

The marker physically survives in `.aider.chat.history.md`: an HTML comment in assistant prose is neither a `####` user line nor a `>` blockquote, so `ParseLineRaw` buffers it as ordinary prose (parser.go:135-143) — confirmed against the recorded markdown format (regressions/multi-turn-conversation transcript). Because the trace exists and the daemon mis-handles it, the rubric routes this to `bug` (file an issue, record `known_failing`), NOT `incapable`. The spec asserts the CORRECT behavior; record files the issue and records known_failing.

## driver_capability: ready (with an explicit human prereq)

VERIFIED FACT: no script under tools/onboarding-factory/scripts/ executes a recipe `setup` array (`grep -n setup run-cell.sh drive-tmux-agent.sh recipe-lint.sh` returns nothing) — the rig has NO instruction-file-seeding primitive, so the recipe cannot create a conventions file fresh each run. The opencode/pi siblings hit gap:seed_instruction for exactly this reason. Aider is the exception that stays `ready`, on a persistent user-level mechanism a human seeds once out-of-band:

- run-cell.sh isolates ONLY the daemon's IRRLICHT_HOME (run-cell.sh:182-236); it never sets `HOME=` and never `env -i`, and its own comment states agents \"watch the real $HOME … regardless of IRRLICHT_HOME\" (run-cell.sh:178-179). The aider drivers launch `aider` plainly (drive-aider-interactive.sh:130 tmux new-session; drive-aider.sh:68 timeout aider) inheriting the ambient env, so aider reads the REAL ~/.aider.conf.yml during the recording run — the same config that already supplies the gpt-5-nano model this cell records against.
- Aider loads a PERSISTENT read-only instruction file via `~/.aider.conf.yml` `read: <CONVENTIONS.md>` (or `aider --read CONVENTIONS.md`), which applies on every invocation (aider.chat/docs/usage/conventions.html). A human seeds ~/.aider.conf.yml + a persistent CONVENTIONS.md ONCE; because $HOME is not isolated, the `read:` entry survives into the rig's run and the marker rule is in context for every turn — no per-run rig seeding needed.

The recipe steps themselves need only `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep`, all implemented by the interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh DRIVE_ELICITS=\"send slash wait_turn interrupt sleep\"), so no new step type is required; the prompts still cue each round so completed_rounds advances. Interactive (tmux REPL) over headless `--message` for the same reason as the sibling aider cells: aider's working→ready settle is IdleFlush-synthesized (~1.5s quiet, clamped to the ~5s SessionDetector poll), so the process must outlive the observation window for the suppress-at-ready phase to be observed.

PREREQ (human, one-time): seed ~/.aider.conf.yml with a `read:` entry pointing at a persistent CONVENTIONS.md that states the irrlicht-eta marker rule. This is an out-of-band human step (like aider auth), NOT a rig step — record assumes it is done.

## Why this is not incapable / not a downgrade

The 3-state model has no trouble here — the estimate rides on metrics during `working` and the arc is the ordinary ready→working→ready. The only thing missing is parser code. Mislabeling this `incapable` would route the fix to the wrong owner (architecture) instead of the adapter.
caveats: PROVISIONAL emission risk (keeps confidence at 0.75): the recorded aider setup uses gpt-5-nano via ~/.aider.conf.yml; aider's terse, diff-focused output style plus a small model make periodic, format-stable marker emission uncertain. The 2026-05-31 claudecode experiment already measured 0% EXACT-format compliance (the model rewrites the marker), which is why the parser is tolerant — but a model that omits the marker entirely emits nothing to parse. record promotes this from provisional to settled.
Even after the parser bug is fixed, an aider scan must read the FULL buffered prose in closeModelCall before truncation; reusing the truncated AssistantText would silently lose markers in long turns.

driver=ready RESTS on the human prereq: the rig has no setup/seed primitive (verified — no script runs a recipe `setup` array), so the marker rule must arrive via the persistent ~/.aider.conf.yml `read: CONVENTIONS.md` path that survives into the run because run-cell.sh does not isolate the agent's $HOME. The prompts still cue each round, but the FORMAT rule lives in the seeded conventions file. If a maintainer prefers a rig-owned seed instead of the out-of-band file, that is a driver enhancement (a seed step, or aider_extra_args ["--read","CONVENTIONS.md"] plus a workspace file-write) — and absent BOTH the human prereq and that enhancement the cell would degrade to gap:seed_instruction like its opencode/pi siblings.

sources.note: scanTaskEstimate/ev.TaskEstimate wired ONLY in the claudecode parser — the v1 marker scan
scan reads FULL block text because AssistantText is tail-truncated to 200 runes
aider closeModelCall emits truncate(text) (last 200 runes) and never calls any task-estimate scan — no ev.TaskEstimate
an HTML-comment marker in assistant prose is buffered as ordinary prose (not #### / not >), so it DOES land in .aider.chat.history.md
recorded aider markdown confirms assistant prose lands as plain lines between #### and > Tokens:
aider persistent-instruction mechanism: aider --read CONVENTIONS.md and .aider.conf.yml read: entry
driver DRIVE_ELICITS=send slash wait_turn interrupt sleep — recipe steps all present, driver=ready
observations verify supports only model/cost_nonzero/tokens_nonzero — task_estimate asserted via phases/notes

preconditions: aider CLI on PATH and authenticated against a model that actually answers (auth set up out-of-band). The recorded setup uses gpt-5-nano via ~/.aider.conf.yml.
tmux available — the interactive driver runs aider in a tmux REPL so it stays alive across turns (the working→ready settle is idle-flush-bound and must be observed before teardown).
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record.
setup: The interactive driver git-inits a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/ and launches `aider --no-auto-commits --yes-always --no-gitignore` (plus --model if IRRLICHT_AIDER_MODEL is set) in a tmux session, waiting for the `> Repo-map:` banner before sending the first prompt.

No driver seeds a persistent instruction file today, so the marker rule is folded into the FIRST send prompt: instruct aider to plan a 3-round task and, on each turn, include in its prose a hidden marker EXACTLY of the form (fenced so the model sees the literal HTML comment): `<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":M} -->`, advancing completed_rounds 0→1→2→3 as each round completes.

KNOWN FAILING (daemon=bug): the aider adapter has no TaskEstimate scan and truncates AssistantText to 200 runes, so even though the marker lands in .aider.chat.history.md the daemon emits no task_estimate. This recipe records the live behavior; the spec asserts the CORRECT (claudecode-equivalent) behavior and record files the parser-bug issue.

script.text: You will do a 3-round planning task. On EVERY reply, include this exact hidden marker in your prose, updating completed_rounds as you go: <!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":0} -->. Round 1: list three subtasks for adding a CHANGELOG.md to this repo. Emit the marker with completed_rounds:0 now, then reply.
Round 1 done. Now do Round 2: draft the CHANGELOG.md header section as text (do not write a file). Update the marker to completed_rounds:1 in this reply.
Round 2 done. Now Round 3: draft an example entry under the header. Update the marker to completed_rounds:2 in this reply.
Round 3 done — the task is complete. Confirm completion and emit the marker with completed_rounds:3.
